title: danthonia racemosa var. penicillata
intro: Danthonia racemosa var. penicillata, also known as Poverty Grass or Mountain Brome, is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Danthonia racemosa var. penicillata include reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and soothing sore throats. Traditionally, herbal preparations of the plant were made by infusing the dried leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using it in combination with other herbs to create a decoction.
